nyheter.title: Lawmakers push for domestic shipbuilding fixes as US Navy explores overseas options

nyheter.title: Norway Cancels Kongsberg-Malaysian Government Contract for Naval Strike Missiles, Launchers
nyheter.summary: Norway canceled a contract between Kongsberg and Malaysia for Naval Strike Missiles and its launchers after the Nordic country implemented new arms restrictions. Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim protested the decision on Thursday. Malaysia was set to procure the Naval Strike Missiles and associated equipment from the Norwegian-based contractor for six Maharaja Lela-class Littoral Combat Ships under a $145 million contract signed in 2018. nyheter.summary: Aircraft from Carrier Air Wing 8 embarked on the Ford returned to their home naval air stations on Monday.

nyheter.title: Virginia Subs Will Hit 2-A-Year Build Rate in 2030s, CNO Caudle Says
nyheter.summary: Shipbuilders are on track to deliver two Virginia-class attack submarines per year in the early 2030s, Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told defense appropriators on Tuesday. nyheter.title: Marine Corps Revamps Training Courses to Build Reconnaissance, Scout Skills
nyheter.summary: The Marine Corps’ renowned reconnaissance training is being revamped to better prepare reconnaissance Marines and infantry scouts for the tech-driven, multi-domain fight on land and at sea. The 12-week Basic Reconnaissance Course will be replaced by two courses – Ground Reconnaissance course and Amphibious Reconnaissance course – and with new infantry training requirements, officials told reporters Monday during a media conference call. nyheter.title: Sinking Ships in the South China Sea
nyheter.summary: PAOAY, Philippines — An audible click preceded the plumes of exhaust that the two Japanese anti-ship missiles left in their wake. The Type 88 missiles unfolded their wings and cruised toward the ship at a steady subsonic speed, finding their mark six minutes after launch. The target: BRP Quezon (PS-70), a World War II-era corvette formerly used by the Philippine Navy located around 46 miles off Northern Luzon. The first missile plowed into the superstructure. nyheter.summary: The new plan comes after President Donald Trump and previous Navy Secretary John Phelan reportedly disagreed over outsourcing construction on select ships.

nyheter.title: 10-ship Russian Convoy Transits Japan’s Tsushima Strait
nyheter.summary: A 10-ship Russian convoy transited Japan’s Tsushima Strait to enter the East China Sea over the weekend, according to Japan’s Joint Staff Office. The Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) spotted two Russian corvettes, a fleet oiler, a tugboat and six cargo ships around 5 p.m. Saturday sailing southwest 140 kilometers northeast of Tsushima, and from Saturday to Sunday, the convoy transited the strait, reads a Monday news release from Japan’s Joint Staff Office. nyheter.title: 2 Dead, 1 Survivor in Friday Strike on Suspected Narco Boat
nyheter.summary: U.S. forces struck a suspected narco boat Friday, killing two men and leaving one survivor, U.S. Southern Command announced. The boat was operating in the Eastern Pacific and is alleged to have been transiting along known narco-trafficking routes when it was struck. Following the strike, SOUTHCOM notified the U.S. Coast Guard of the survivor. The Coast Guard had not announced an update in the search and rescue of the survivor as of early Monday. The task group included submarine RFS Petropavlovsk-Kamchatsky (B-274), corvette RFS Gromkiy (335) and tugboat Andrei Stepanov. nyheter.title: Future Aircraft Carrier Doris Miller Delayed by 2 Years
nyheter.summary: One of the Navy’s Ford-class aircraft carriers is delayed by two years, stretching the schedule to build the future USS Doris Miller (CVN-81) out to 15 years, USNI News has learned. Carrier Doris Miller will now deliver to the Navy in February of 2034, according to the service’s Fiscal Year 2027 budget justification books. nyheter.title: Strait of Hormuz Shipping in State of Confusion as Industry Watches U.S., Iranian Actions
nyheter.summary: Three Navy destroyers came under fire from Iran as they transited the Strait of Hormuz as commercial shipping traffic remains at an almost standstill. Iranian forces sent missiles, drones and small boats after USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) and USS Truxton (DDG-103) as the three warships headed toward the Gulf of Oman, according to U.S. Central Command news release. rov_teknologi.title: Iran’s Plan to Charge for Strait of Hormuz Transits Could Spread to Other Regions, Experts Warn
rov_teknologi.summary: Iran’s push to charge merchant shippers for passage through the Strait of Hormuz under threat of violence could spread to other parts of the world, maritime experts said this week. The problem arises if there is any normalization of Iran’s actions in creating a passageway that requires ships to provide their information, including ownerships, and a potential fee to cross, Ian Ralby, a non-resident senior fellow at the Center for Maritime Security, said during a Thursday Lloyd’s List webinar. rov_teknologi.summary: The new Trump-class battleship will be powered by a nuclear reactor and is not a successor to the Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyers, the Navy revealed Monday in its annual 30-year shipbuilding blueprint.
